questions.prompt: Script theory is most useful for explaining:
options: Why all relationships develop identically regardless of culture
How prescribed social roles shape expectations in romantic relationships
Why attraction is determined only by biology
Why conflict is lower in all 2SLGBTQ+ relationships

questions.prompt: Which finding about relationship formation in 2SLGBTQ+ relationships was emphasized in lecture?
options: They never begin as friendships
They often begin as friendships before becoming romantic
They follow the exact same gendered courtship scripts as heterosexual relationships in every case
They are less likely to involve emotional intimacy

questions.prompt: Lecture material on domestic labour and power in 2SLGBTQ+ relationships suggests that:
options: Equality is never valued in these relationships
Partners often value equality, even if actual divisions may not always be perfectly equal
One partner is always expected to take the traditionally feminine role
Power differences are always greater than in heterosexual relationships

questions.prompt: Which statement is most consistent with lecture material on satisfaction and conflict in 2SLGBTQ+ relationships?
options: They are entirely different from heterosexual relationships on all major measures
They are pretty similar on relationship satisfaction and conflict overall
They consistently show no conflict because gender scripts are absent
They are always less stable because of minority stress

questions.prompt: The Minority Stress Model proposes that poor health outcomes among marginalized groups can arise because:
options: Minority identities directly cause low relationship quality
Distal stressors can lead to proximal stressors, which together affect health and well-being
Only internal stress matters
Family support has no role in coping

questions.prompt: Which of the following is the best example of a distal stressor in the Minority Stress Model?
options: Internalized shame about one's identity
Anticipating rejection from others
Experiencing discrimination or victimization from society
Wanting emotional intimacy with a partner

questions.prompt: Which coping response discussed in lecture would be most likely to buffer minority stress?
options: Self-acceptance and support from chosen family
Greater adherence to rigid gender scripts
Avoiding all close relationships
Reducing partner communication

questions.prompt: Lecture material on children in 2SLGBTQ+ families most strongly suggests:
options: Children fare worse because they lack traditional gender-role models
There is no evidence that children are inherently harmed by having 2SLGBTQ+ parents
Research is impossible because these families are too rare to study
Children do well only when raised by two-parent heterosexual households

questions.prompt: Which statement best reflects the lecture discussion of transgender experiences in relationships?
options: There is already more than enough research, so major questions are settled
Transition during a relationship may introduce stigma-related challenges, and partner support can matter greatly
Findings from all other relationship research definitely do not apply at all
Transgender experiences are unrelated to minority stress

questions.prompt: Which statement best matches the lecture discussion of relationship dissolution in 2SLGBTQ+ relationships?
options: We know everything important already, including complete long-term timelines
Relationship dissolution is impossible to compare across groups
The evidence base is still somewhat limited, including limited long-term timelines
2SLGBTQ+ couples never remain friends after breakups
